core_mechanism: linear (dispersion-relation) stability analysis of a spatially uniform state in a self-interacting nonlinear medium, in which a feedback between local amplitude and the medium's nonlinear response amplifies a band of periodic perturbations, with the width and location of the unstable-wavenumber band determined by the statistical spectral distribution (degree of incoherence) of the underlying fluctuations
brief_reason: The abstract analyzes when a partially coherent field in a nonlinear Kerr medium is modulationally unstable and shows the set of unstable wave numbers and the presence/absence of instability depend crucially on the profile of the incoherent (statistical) field spectrum, i.e. a self-amplification stability threshold whose unstable band is controlled by the fluctuation spectrum.
